title: Public Share Player — Unauthenticated Clip Playback
problem: A frozen clip should be shareable outside the band — with a producer, a friend, or social media. The listener should not need a Google account or any signup. Only the frozen render is exposed; no session metadata, band data, or version history leaks through the public page.
analysis:
The public share page (/share/[token]) fetches clip metadata by publicToken from Postgres. Audio is served via /api/audio/public/[token] — a separate proxy route that looks up the clip by token and streams the finalDriveFileId from Drive using the recorder's stored token. No user auth is required on either the page or the audio route.

PublicPlayer.tsx uses WaveSurfer.js (interact: false) to render the waveform and play the frozen audio. Stamp chips are rendered via StampJumpRow — tappable buttons that seek to their timestamp on tap, consistent with WaveformPlayer and WaveformEditor. WaveformPlayButton renders the shared amber play/pause button. A download button triggers the /api/audio/public/[token] route with a download intent, giving the user the .aac file.

The public page shows: clip name, band name (optional), clip duration, waveform player with stamps, play/pause, download. No version list, no vote buttons, no comments, no edit actions — nothing that reveals internal band structure.
successCriteria:
- /share/[token] loads without authentication
- Waveform renders and plays the frozen render audio
- Stamp chips displayed with correct emoji and timestamp
- Play/pause 64px button — large touch target
- Download button downloads the .aac file
- Page shows clip name and duration
- Error state shown if token is invalid or clip has been un-shared
- Audio not exposed if publicToken is null or clip is not frozen
implementation:
1. components/share/PublicPlayer.tsx: client component. Props: token, sourceDurationMs, stamps[]. Uses useWaveSurfer({ url: /api/audio/public/{token}, interact: false — no cutMarksRef, no patchDurationUrl, public read-only }). Uses WaveformPlayButton for play/pause. Uses StampJumpRow for stamp chips (tappable, seek on tap) with custom className for layout. No WaveformCanvas overlay — no editing or seeking via waveform drag needed (play-only).
2. app/share/[token]/page.tsx: server component. Fetches clip by publicToken from Postgres (clips table). If not found or clip.frozen=false: returns 404 page. Renders clip name, duration, PublicPlayer with stamps.
3. app/api/audio/public/[token]/route.ts: streams audio from Drive. Looks up clip by publicToken, gets finalDriveFileId, fetches recorder's token from NextAuth accounts table, proxies Drive stream with Range header support. No auth middleware — open to public traffic.

decisions.decision: Audio served via server-side proxy even on public pages
decisions.rationale: Even with Drive public permission enabled on the final file, the Drive URL format includes the file ID which reveals internal structure. Proxying via /api/audio/public/[token] keeps the only public identifier as the opaque token, allows revocation (clear token → proxy returns 404), and avoids exposing file IDs in the page source.
decisions.recommendation: GET /api/audio/public/{token} → look up clip by token → stream Drive file via recorder's stored token. Supports Range requests. Returns 404 if token is null or clip not frozen.

decisions.decision: No version history, no collaboration data on the public page
decisions.rationale: The public page is for external listeners — producers, fans, collaborators outside the band. Exposing version numbers, vote tallies, or comments reveals internal band workflow details. The public page is intentionally minimal: name, waveform, stamps, download.
decisions.recommendation: Query only: clip.name, clip.sourceDurationMs, clip.finalDriveFileId, stamps. Nothing from votes, comments, band_members, or clip_versions tables.

knownLimitations: Public audio proxy uses the recorder's Drive token — if the recorder revokes access, public links break even for frozen clips
No view analytics — there's no way to know how many people have listened to a shared clip
PublicPlayer renders the waveform but the overlay div is not present — users cannot seek by clicking on the waveform (play/pause only). Seeking via stamps is available.
userGuide: After freezing a clip, tap 'Enable public share' on the clip detail page to generate a /share/[token] link. Share this URL with anyone — they can open it in any browser without signing in. They'll see the clip name, a waveform player, and stamp markers. They can play, seek, and download the audio. To revoke access, tap 'Enable public share' again to disable — the link immediately stops working.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Stamp chips in PublicPlayer are now tappable (StampJumpRow, same as WaveformPlayer). WaveformPlayButton extracted as shared component. Updated interact:false. Removed stale limitation about non-tappable stamps. Added limitation about no waveform-click seeking (no overlay div in PublicPlayer).
